skill.name: pre-commit-check
skill.description: コミット前品質チェックスキル（Lint、テスト、ビルドの高速検証）
skill.summary: コミット前品質チェックスキル（Lint、テスト、ビルドの高速検証）
skill.author: Crearize

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many Ruby backtick hits, but the evidence is Markdown fenced command examples, not executable Ruby source. The weak-crypto and high-entropy findings are false positives in the front matter and Markdown text. The skill still carries medium risk because it instructs agents to run git, Gradle, pnpm, and npx commands from the target repository.

medium_findings.title: Repository Command Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs an agent to run external commands for git status checks, Gradle lint and test tasks, pnpm lint and test scripts, and TypeScript checks. This is expected for a pre-commit quality skill, but repository-controlled scripts and dependencies can execute arbitrary local code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed commands are explicit and central to the skill workflow. The risk is contextual rather than malicious because they are normal development commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported a weak cryptographic algorithm at line 3, but line 3 is the Japanese description field in the skill front matter. No cryptographic algorithm or cryptographic operation is present there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct inspection of the reported line shows descriptive metadata only. I did not find evidence of cryptographic code in the file.

low_findings.title: Entropy Finding Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The high-entropy finding at the start of the file appears to be caused by Markdown front matter and Japanese prose. The file is readable text, not binary, encrypted, or obfuscated content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported location contains plain YAML front matter. The complete file is readable Markdown with no encoded payload.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The command blocks include git, Gradle, pnpm, and npx execution. These commands are legitimate for this skill, but users should run them only in trusted repositories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external command usage is explicit in Markdown examples and referenced tooling paths. There is no evidence of hidden execution outside the documented workflow.

content.value_statement: Developers need quick feedback before committing code, without running every project check. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through focused lint, test, and compile checks.
actual_capabilities: Reviews changed and staged files with git status commands.
Runs backend lint checks with Gradle Checkstyle and SpotBugs tasks.
Runs targeted backend tests when tests are not skipped.
Runs frontend lint, changed-file tests, and TypeScript type checks.
Reports pass or fail status for backend and frontend checks.
limitations: It does not run the full pull request quality workflow.
It does not enforce test coverage thresholds.
It depends on project-specific backend and frontend command availability.
It can execute repository-defined scripts, so repositories should be trusted.

use_cases.title: Check Work Before Commit
use_cases.description: Run focused checks on changed files before creating a commit.

use_cases.title: Validate Backend Changes
use_cases.description: Run Gradle lint, related tests, and compile checks after Java changes.

use_cases.title: Validate Frontend Changes
use_cases.description: Run lint, changed-file tests, and TypeScript checks before pushing UI changes.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Pre-Commit Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the pre-commit check for my current changes. Include backend and frontend results in the final summary.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a normal local commit.

prompt_templates.title: Skip Tests For Urgent Fix
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the pre-commit check with tests skipped. Explain which tests should be run later.
prompt_templates.scenario: Fast validation when a developer needs lint and compile feedback only.

prompt_templates.title: Backend Focused Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the pre-commit check for backend changes only. Prioritize lint, related tests, and compilation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Targeted backend verification before committing Java changes.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Failures And Next Steps
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the pre-commit check, group any failures by area, and recommend the smallest next fixes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for triaging multiple quality failures.

output_examples.input: Run a pre-commit check on staged backend and frontend files.
output_examples.output: The result separates backend and frontend checks, marks lint, test, compile, and type-check status, and states whether the commit is ready.

output_examples.input: Run the check but skip tests for an urgent change.
output_examples.output: The result lists completed lint and compile checks, notes that tests were skipped, and names follow-up test work.

output_examples.input: Explain why the pre-commit check failed.
output_examples.output: The result summarizes failed areas, lists the first errors to fix, and recommends rerunning the focused check after changes.

best_practices: Run the skill from the repository root so relative paths resolve correctly.
Use test skipping only for urgent cases and run tests afterward.
Run the full QA workflow before opening a pull request.
anti_patterns: Do not run repository scripts from untrusted codebases.
Do not treat this lightweight check as a coverage gate.
Do not ignore failed lint, compile, or type-check results.

faq.question: Does this replace a full QA check?
faq.answer: No. It is a lightweight pre-commit check. The skill says to run the full QA check before a pull request.

faq.question: Can it skip tests?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow allows skipped tests for urgent cases, but skipped tests should be run later.

faq.question: Which backend tools does it reference?
faq.answer: It references Gradle tasks for Checkstyle, SpotBugs, targeted tests, and Java compilation.

faq.question: Which frontend tools does it reference?
faq.answer: It references pnpm lint checks, changed-file tests, and TypeScript type checking.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a warning. It runs external development commands, so users should use trusted repositories.

faq.question: Does the audit find prompt injection?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed file contains normal workflow instructions and command examples.
